title: Author founding OrgMissionStatementModel for every substrate-owned org
description:
Follow-on to clia-org's founding mission landing this session (commit a7e768e8 in clia-org / 927c1681cc in mono). Author one founding-mission JSON record per substrate-owned org under each org's `private/universal/identity/<org>@rismay.substrate.mission.json` (or equivalent identity dir).

SCOPE — substrate-owned orgs surveyed at authoring time (2026-05-26). Exact list to be re-confirmed at execution since the substrate grows continuously:

FACTORIES (digikoma/agent/ghost manufacturers + dispatchers):
- digikoma-org (manufactures .digikoma.clia bundles; the workers)
- ghost-shell-org (chassis + per-instance ghost bundles; the human-replica side)
- koma-org (related to digikoma-org? — confirm at authoring)

SCHEMA + TYPED-CONTRACT LAYERS:
- schema-universal (the substrate's typed-contract home; family: schema-family)
- service-universal (typed service contracts; sibling to schema-universal)

PRIMITIVE LIBRARIES:
- wrkstrm-core (common-* primitives; ResourceQueue, common-service, common-imprint, common-process, common-shell, common-cli, etc.)
- swift-universal (cross-cutting Swift libraries; SwiftUniversalGitCore, common-process, swift-git-cli, etc.)
- swift-gen-ui-universal
- swift-ui-universal

AGENT-SKILL COLLECTIVES (substrate-native agent skill bundles):
- swift-agent-skills
- swift-concurrency-agent-skill
- swift-testing-agent-skill
- swiftdata-agent-skill
- swiftui-agent-skill

APPS:
- clia-app-org (clia-agents app + related)
- wrkstrm-app (App Store presence; hack-nu + others)
- tau-app
- laussat-studio
- switch-on-day
- text-allora

ORG-SHAPED COLLECTIVES:
- atlas-org
- kura-org
- slate-org
- vapor-wares-org / vapor-wares (confirm relationship at authoring)
- vaults-org~HEAD (octopus-merge artifact in path; resolve before authoring)

INFRASTRUCTURE:
- codeswiftly (publishing/distribution; the substrate's package-publishing layer)
- todo3 (parent of multiple vaults + the commissioned-collective pointer)
- wrkstrm (the founding org; substrate-wide)
- wrkstrm-components, wrkstrm-finance, wrkstrm-games, wrkstrm-metal-components, wrkstrm-performance, wrkstrm-research (specialized wrkstrm-family orgs)

EXCLUDE (external; don't author):
- anthropic, google, openai, paperclip-ai (vendor SDKs/forks)
- noze-io, pointfreeco (external upstreams we vendor)
- pi (third-party badlogic per substrate memory)
- openclaw (mixed — check before authoring)
- public (utility dir, not an org)

AUTHORING PROTOCOL FOR EACH ORG:
1. Read the org's existing identity envelope (brand.json / collective.json / agent.triad.json) to extract the implicit mission from prose descriptions
2. Distill to a one-line ikigai (mission field)
3. Identify productSlugs[] — what does this org actually ship/operate? Use the typed catalog slugs where they exist (digikoma/agent/ghost from CLIAKindOrdinalityTable; schema-family, common-primitive, app, vault, etc. as open vocabulary otherwise)
4. Identify serves[] — LinkRefs to who this org's outputs go to (other orgs, operators, the substrate-as-whole)
5. establishedDate = best-known origin date for the org (check git log of first commit if unknown)
6. supersedes = null for founding (every entry in this sweep is a founding mission)
7. Author at <org-root>/private/universal/identity/<org>@rismay.substrate.mission.json
8. Commit per-org with focused message; bump mono pointer in a batched pointer-bump commit at the end

NOT-NEGOTIABLE per substrate doctrine:
- One mission record per org. No prose-only descriptions in lieu of typed records.
- Schema discipline: each record uses the substrate class-name discriminator pattern; decoder rejects on schema mismatch.
- Supersession over edit-in-place: if this sweep's first authoring needs correction later, it gets superseded by a new record, not mutated.
- Don't sweep other-session dirty state in target submodules. Per-org commit must be surgical add of only the new mission file.

OUT OF SCOPE (separate beads):
- Mission statements for agents/operators/ghosts (org-mission-statement-schemas may graduate to entity-mission-statement-schemas at that point — see the v0.1.0 docstring about the upward generalization path)
- Mission supersession workflows (when orgs pivot, the new mission authors against the prior — needs operator decision per pivot)
- Tooling that renders missions in roster / fleet view (separate bead in clia-bundle-suffix-grammar-substrate-adoption arc; the data needs to exist first)

ESTIMATED EFFORT: ~3-5 min per org × ~35 orgs = ~2-3 hours of focused authoring. Can fan out across multiple sessions or batch in one. Operator's choice on cadence.
